LAW & MOTION/DISCOVERY, DEPT. 301, AS TO THE OCT-16-2025 HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF MEI LIU'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEPOSITION OF LISA KELLEY; THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING WITH THE MODIFICATION THAT THE AMENDED NOTICE IS THE APPLICABLE NOTICE FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, AND THAT THE DEPONENT DOES NOT NEED TO PRODUCE THE PROPERTY TAXES PERTAINING TO CATEGORY 10. THE TENTATIVE IS OTHERWISE ADOPTED. COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH CONFORMS TO THE RULING. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; CLERK: V. DA FONSECA; NOT REPORTED. Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF'S EX PARTE APPLICATION FOR AN ORDER SHORTENING TIME TO HEAR MOTION TO COMPEL DEPOSITION OF DEFENDANT VERITAS INVESTMENTS, INC. VICE PRESIDENT LISA KELLEY, DEMAND FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, AND FOR SANCTIONS

Matter not argued / contested. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The hearing on Plaintiff Mei Liu's motion to compel deposition of Lisa Kelly and for monetary sanctions will occur on October 16, 2025. Though the motion was served with insufficient notice, by opposing the motion on the merits Veritas waived the untimeliness of the motion. "[O]pposition to the motion on its merits is a waiver of any defects or irregularities in the notice of motion," particularly where, as here, the opposing party has not claimed, much less shown, any prejudice. (Carlton v. Quint (2000) 77 Cal. App. 4th 690, 697-698 (quoting an earlier case)).

Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/HEK)

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which conforms to the ruling.
